landing.title: Project Off The Books
landing.tagline: Welcome to the only project at Verve that will never be audited.
landing.subtitle: Knocking was the easy part. What's behind the door, no one knows.

vibe.helper: this is data we will absolutely never use
vibe.closeTitle: That's enough conspiracy for one day.
vibe.closeSubtitle: Whatever it actually is — you're early. Step through.
vibe.closer: Is this what's behind the door?

rumours.heading: A Giant Came Knocking
body: A name you'd recognise has been quietly calling — one of the big multinational consultancies — not to send us work, but to put their name beside ours.
This isn't about who's bigger. It's two firms deciding they're worth more together than apart.
The real question isn't whether it happens — it's what changes for the rest of us when it does.

rumours.heading: An Address in the Valley
rumours.sub: Are we opening a Verve branch in Silicon Valley?
body: "San Francisco" keeps turning up — on a whiteboard nobody will explain, in calls that happen at odd hours.
A US office isn't about prestige. It's a firm deciding it's done flying to a market and ready to live in it.
Whatever's being set up out west, the interesting part isn't the address — it's who gets sent to sit at it.

rumours.heading: AI, for Every CA in the Country
rumours.sub: An ICAI initiative to put AI in the whole profession's hands?
body: Word is there's a conversation happening with the institute itself — not about one firm's tools, but the profession's.
Helping every CA work with AI isn't a product. It's a position: becoming the name attached to the shift before anyone else claims it.
If it's real, the question isn't whether the profession changes — it's who's standing at the front of the room when it does.

rumours.heading: Bali Is Calling
rumours.sub: An international workation — the thing that's topped everyone's wishlist for years?
body: Picture it: the whole firm working out of a villa, stand-ups by the pool, deadlines that somehow feel lighter with the ocean in earshot.
Word is it might be more than a daydream this time — maybe someone should start a list of who's actually got a valid passport.
Sunset team dinners, a beach instead of a boardroom, stories nobody back home will believe. If it's real, this is the trip we'll never stop talking about.

rumours.heading: A School That Doesn't Exist Yet
rumours.sub: An academy for finance and AI — staff swear it's real; HR's gone quiet.
body: Every firm says it's "investing in AI." Almost none turn that into a building, a syllabus, and a name over the door.
An academy isn't a training budget — it's a firm deciding to grow its own talent instead of hunting for it.
If it's real, the question isn't what they'll teach. It's what they're quietly preparing us for.

rumours.heading: From Sambhaji Nagar to Europe?
rumours.sub: Word is VerviGreen's indoor saffron farm is crossing a border.
body: Priya's saffron project was never meant to be ordinary — growing the world's costliest spice indoors, while everyone insisted it couldn't scale.
A year of headlines later, the whisper is a second farm. In Europe. The kind of move you only make when the first one quietly worked.
The question isn't whether VerviGreen can grow saffron. It's how far one out-of-the-box idea is allowed to travel.

rumours.heading: When a Client Becomes a Partner
rumours.sub: A joint venture to move into SAP implementation and migration?
body: We've advised on systems for years. The whisper is we're about to start building and migrating them — the actual heavy lifting, not just the blueprint.
Stranger still: the partner is said to be one of our own clients. The line between who we serve and who we build with is apparently about to blur.
A move like this isn't a new service line. It's Verve deciding advice was only ever step one.

rumours.heading: New Vervians, New Time Zones
rumours.sub: Is Verve starting to hire across borders?
body: Word is the next wave of Vervians might not share our postcode — or our passport. Roles are reportedly opening across borders and time zones.
Picture it: new colleagues, new accents, new festivals on the calendar, a stand-up that spans continents — a team that swaps recipes as easily as it swaps spreadsheets.

rumours.heading: Lights, Camera, Cashflow
rumours.sub: Is Verve launching its own high-production finance podcast?
body: Word is there's a studio being built — proper lights, proper cameras — for a finance show with production values nobody expects from an advisory firm.
Big names and sharp minds in the guest chair, real conversations, glossy episodes you'd actually queue up on a Sunday. Verve with its own show, its own voice, its own audience.
Run by Vervians, naturally. If it's real, we stop being the firm with good opinions and become the one with a microphone.

rumours.heading: A Tool That Finally Fits
rumours.sub: Are we building our own project-management tool — and ditching the clunky one?
body: Every firm runs on software it secretly resents — too many tabs, too many fields nobody fills in. Word is Verve's done resenting it and started building its own.
Not another dashboard to wrestle. A tool shaped around how we actually work, by the people who actually do the work.
If it's real, Verve quietly crosses a line — from a firm that uses software to one that makes it.

rumours.heading: Pitch Your Wildest Idea
rumours.sub: A monthly "Verve Tank" where anyone can pitch — and the boldest ideas get built?
body: The rumour: once a month, a room, a stage, and anyone with an idea too big for a Tuesday meeting. Pitch it — properly — to people who can actually say yes.
Not a suggestion box that vanishes into the void. Real backing, real budgets, a genuine "let's build it" for the ideas that land.
If it's true, "I've got an idea" stops being a daydream — and becomes a slot on next month's stage.

rumours.heading: Our Name on a Product
rumours.sub: Is Verve launching its own AI-powered product this year?
body: For years we've built things for clients and handed them over. The whisper is that this time we're building something that stays ours — an AI product with Verve's name on the box.
Not a feature, not a side project. A real launch, this year, aimed at the market — the kind of thing that turns an advisory firm into a product company overnight.
If it's true, the question isn't what it does. It's what Verve becomes the day it ships.

rumours.heading: VerviFit 4.0?
rumours.sub: The rumour: VerviFit's next leap — snap one photo, and AI builds your whole plan.
body: VerviFit has always nudged us to stay fit, even from our desks at home. Now the whisper is its boldest leap yet — and it's powered by AI.

Click one photo of yourself, and the rest is handled: a routine made for your body, a diet plan that fits your life — plus little nudges through the day to move, breathe, and actually take that break.
If it's real, staying fit stops being something you figure out — and becomes something VerviFit just hands you.

rumours.heading: A Workation That Floats
rumours.sub: Are we doing our workation on a cruise?
body: Forget a resort that stays in one place. The whisper is a ship — the whole firm aboard, working by day, the ocean moving under us, a new port every morning.
Decks instead of meeting rooms. Sunsets that are different every night. The kind of "out of office" that's quite literally somewhere else by the time you wake up.
If it's real, "where do you work?" gets the best answer it's ever had: international waters.

rumours.heading: A Coach Who Never Logs Off
rumours.sub: Is everyone getting a full-time AI coach for professional growth?
body: Not an app you forget you downloaded. The rumour is an always-on coach for every Vervian — there the moment you're stuck, learning right alongside you.
Guidance tailored to you: the answer when you need it, the next thing worth picking up, a steady push to get a little sharper at what you do.
If it's real, growing at work stops being something you chase on your own — and becomes something that's with you at every step.

rumours.heading: From Boardroom to Bookshelf
rumours.sub: Are the founders publishing a book on modern advisory and business growth?
body: The rumour is a proper, on-the-shelf book — years of how Verve actually thinks about advisory and growth, finally written down.
Not a whitepaper nobody reads. A real title with the founders' names on the spine, the kind of thing that lands on a CEO's desk and a junior's reading list alike.
If it's real, the playbook we live by stops being word-of-mouth — and becomes something the whole industry can hold.

signIn.title: There's Only One Way In.
signIn.subtitle: We won't tell you which one is the real project. You'll have to come inside and see for yourself.
body: Everyone is guessing. Almost no one will know — until they knock.
It will ask who you are. Give it only the name Verve gave you.
signIn.checkEmail: We emailed a 6-digit code to {email}. Enter it below — the code keeps you right here (no need to leave the page).
signIn.fine: Off the record, naturally. Use your Verve account.

brief.title: If you choose to accept it.
brief.quote: You knocked, you guessed, you came in. Now the part that's actually off the books: the people you're looking for were never the ones beside you. We took finance, HR, ops and IT and quietly reshuffled the deck. Find them not by where they sit, but by the quirks and little legends only their crew would know. Light up every canister — and what's behind the door stops being a rumour.

guess.title: Guess your crew.
guess.subtitle: Read each clue and name the colleague it points to. A correct call marks them yellow — but a canister only turns green when you and that teammate guess each other.
guess.pendingNote: Guess on {name} is in. Turns green when they name you back. 🟡
guess.confirmedNote: Mutual! {name} named you back. Trust established — canister's green. 🟢
guess.wrongNote: Not quite — read the clue again and try another name.
guess.partDoneSubtitle: Nice work. Your matches turn green the moment those teammates guess you back — watch the wheel.
guess.completeSubtitle: Every canister green. The whole crew guessed each other — that's the actual mission. Stand by, next transmission incoming.
loading: Decrypting your crew… don't tell HR.
Establishing secure channel… scrambling the org chart.
guess.emptyHint: Quiet on the wheel. Pick a node and make the first call.
guess.clickHint: Click a glowing node to decode its canister.
guess.errorBody: Couldn't assemble this crew's wheel. Refresh — and if it persists, ping the host.

wait.title: Now We Wait.
wait.subtitle: The rest stays off the books — until the morning of the 1st.
body: The rumours will keep spinning. Let them — none of them are the whole story.
On the 1st, the door opens for real, and you'll be among the first through it. Watch your inbox. Tell no one.
